title: Malvaviscus longifolius
taxonomy: Malvaviscus longifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Malvaviscus, which belongs to the order Malvales. The plant is native to the Americas and is known for its unique flowers and edible fruits. Malvaviscus longifolius is also known as the 'sabila' or 'kiss me quick' due to its colorful and distinctive flowers. The classification of Malvaviscus longifolius reflects its unique characteristics and its relationships to other plants within the Malvaceae family.
